tools.name: mcp__playwright
tools.description: Tools in the mcp__playwright namespace.

tools.name: browser_run_code_unsafe
tools.description: Run a Playwright code snippet. Unsafe: executes arbitrary JavaScript in the Playwright server process and is RCE-equivalent.

code.description: A JavaScript function containing Playwright code to execute. It will be invoked with a single argument, page, which you can use for any page interaction. For example: `async (page) => { await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' }).click(); return await page.title(); }`

filename.description: Load code from the specified file. If both code and filename are provided, code will be ignored.

tools.name: mcp__node_repl
tools.description:
Use `js` to run JavaScript in the persistent Node-backed kernel. When a skill or prompt says to use `node_repl`, call this server's `js` execution tool. Calls default to a 30000 ms (30 seconds) timeout when `timeout_ms` is omitted. The runtime exposes `nodeRepl.cwd`, `nodeRepl.homeDir`, `nodeRepl.tmpDir`, `nodeRepl.requestMeta`, `nodeRepl.setResponseMeta(...)`, and `await nodeRepl.emitImage(...)`. Top-level bindings persist across `js` calls until `js_reset`; do not redeclare existing `const` or `let` names. Reuse existing bindings, use top-level `var` for reusable state that may be assigned again, or choose a fresh descriptive name. Use `js_add_node_module_dir` before `js` when a skill provides an extra package directory, and use dynamic imports like `await import("playwright")` rather than filesystem paths under `./node_modules`.

Use Cases:
- Control the in-app browser in conjunction with the Browser Plugin.
- Control the Chrome browser in conjunction with the Chrome Plugin. Prefer this method of controlling Chrome over alternatives (such as Computer Use) unless the user explicitly mentions an alternative.

tools.name: js
tools.description: Run JavaScript in a persistent Node-backed kernel with top-level await. This is the JavaScript execution tool for the `node_repl` MCP server; use it whenever instructions say to use `node_repl`, the Node REPL MCP, or run Node REPL code. If `timeout_ms` is omitted, execution times out after 30000 ms (30 seconds); pass a larger `timeout_ms` for slow browser automation or other long-running operations. Use `nodeRepl.cwd`, `nodeRepl.homeDir`, and `nodeRepl.tmpDir` to inspect host paths. Use `nodeRepl.requestMeta` to inspect the current MCP request `_meta` object during a tool call. Use `nodeRepl.setResponseMeta(meta)` to attach top-level MCP result `_meta`; repeated calls shallow-merge object keys for the current tool call. Use `nodeRepl.write(text)` when you want exact text output in the tool result; it writes the string exactly as given and does not append a newline. Prefer it over `console.log(...)` for final output, JSON, or other text you plan to consume programmatically. `console.log(...)` is still useful for ad hoc debugging or object inspection because it formats values and appends line breaks automatically. Use `await nodeRepl.emitImage(imageLike)` to return images; each call adds one image to the outer tool result, so call it multiple times to emit multiple images. Supported image inputs are a data URL, inferred PNG/JPEG/WebP bytes, or `{ bytes, mimeType }`. Saved references to `nodeRepl.write(...)` and `nodeRepl.emitImage(...)` stay reusable across calls, but async callbacks that fire after a call finishes still fail because no exec is active. Top-level bindings persist across calls until `js_reset`. If a call throws, prior bindings remain available and bindings that finished initializing before the throw often remain reusable. For reusable names that may be assigned again later, prefer top-level `var name = ...`; `var` can be redeclared across calls. If you hit `SyntaxError: Identifier 'x' has already been declared`, reuse the existing binding if possible, reassign it only if it was declared with `let` or `var`, or pick a new name instead of resetting immediately; a previous `const x` cannot be changed into `var x`. Use a short `{ ... }` block only for temporary scratch names, and do not wrap an entire call in block scope if you want those names reusable later. Use dynamic imports like `await import("playwright")`, `await import("pkg")`, or `await import("./file.js")`; top-level static `import` is not supported. Import packages by package name after installing them into a directory added with `js_add_node_module_dir`, `NODE_REPL_NODE_MODULE_DIRS`, or the working directory. Do not import package entrypoints by filesystem path such as `./node_modules/playwright/index.mjs`. Imported local files must be ESM `.js` or `.mjs` files and run in the context chosen at their dynamic-import boundary, so they can also use `nodeRepl.*`, the captured `console`, and `import.meta` helpers. Bare package imports always resolve from the REPL-wide search roots (`NODE_REPL_NODE_MODULE_DIRS`, then directories later added with `js_add_node_module_dir`, then cwd), not relative to the imported file's location. Imported local files may statically import other local `.js` / `.mjs` files, available packages, and allowed Node builtins. `import.meta.resolve()` returns importable strings such as `file://...`, bare package names, and `node:...` specifiers. Local file modules reload between execs. `node:` builtins are generally available via dynamic import, but `process` / `node:process` remains blocked for now because the current Rust-server-to-Node-child transport runs over stdio and raw process streams can corrupt it. Prefer `nodeRepl.write(text)` for text output and `nodeRepl.emitImage(...)` for images.

code.description: JavaScript source to execute in the persistent Node-backed kernel. The code runs with top-level await and can use the `nodeRepl` helpers. Examples: `nodeRepl.write(nodeRepl.cwd)`, `const { chromium } = await import("playwright")`, or `await nodeRepl.emitImage(pngBuffer)`.

timeout_ms.description: Optional execution timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 30000 (30 seconds) when omitted.

title.description: Short user-facing description of what this code block is doing. Use a few words, for example `Inspect package metadata` or `Render chart preview`.

tools.name: codex_app
tools.description: Tools provided by the Codex app.

tools.name: list_threads
tools.description: List recent Codex threads across the local host and connected remote hosts. Use an optional query to find a specific thread before reading or steering it.

limit.description: Maximum number of thread summaries to return.

tools.name: mcp__devops_mcp
tools.description:
This DevOps MCP intentionally exposes a small always-on MCP tool surface:
health, list_capabilities, get_capability_details, tool_search, and invoke_tool.

For every host, Docker, systemd, file, or audit-log task, call tool_search first
to find the right hidden operation. Then call invoke_tool with the exact operation
name and an args object matching the operation description. Do not assume direct
tools such as run_command, docker_ps, read_file, or service_status are listed in
tools/list; they are discoverable operations to keep client context small.

Call list_capabilities to browse by category or safety class. Call
get_capability_details for one operation's full contract, including args and
examples. Call health first when you need server context, visible tools,
registered agents, or the complete operation-name list. Treat write-capable
operations with care: this server has root-level host access, so prefer
inspection commands before state-changing commands.

tools.name: tool_search
tools.description:
Search the hidden DevOps operation registry by keyword.
Call this first for every host, Docker, file, service, or audit task. Then
call invoke_tool with the exact returned operation name and an args object.

tools.name: list_capabilities
tools.description:
Browse the hidden DevOps operation catalog without invoking anything.
Optional filters: category (docker, files, systemd, audit, host, system) and
safety (read_only, state_changing, destructive). Returns compact contracts;
call get_capability_details for full details on one operation.

It is the authoritative orientation document for any developer or AI session working on this repository. > **Supabase migration:** Cloud SQL → Supabase work is governed by the workspace-wide [`SUPABASE-MIGRATION.md`](../designflow-backend/SUPABASE-MIGRATION.md). Keep using the BFF/backend APIs — do **not** add `supabase-js` or direct Supabase table access for existing features unless explicitly asked, and never put Supabase service-role keys in browser code. --- ## 1. What this project is Designflow is a product-lifecycle-management (PLM) web application used by sourcing, merchandising, licensing, and production teams to manage the full journey of a product from concept to retail. The core workflows are: **RFQ** (request for quote — collect vendor pricing and compute landed-cost and margin chains), **Item Library** (product master data), **Standardized Products**, **Art Piece** management, and **Production/Licensing Tracking**. This repository is the Angular frontend only; the backend REST API is a separate service at `api.designflow.app`. The business-critical outcome is correct margin and landed-cost calculations in the RFQ grid — errors there directly affect buy prices and retailer quotes. --- ## 2. Multi-model AI note There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools. `.claudeignore` works for Claude Code. When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section. --- ## 2a. If a change affects Cloud Run topology, Cloud Build/deploy behavior, domains, BFF routing, secrets/env handling, storage, auth/OIDC, or database operating rules, update that standards page in the same Reskin over the `designflow-tracking` `/api/tracking/sample` API | | `src/app/pages/sample_tracking/helpers/sample.status.ts` | **Our code** — frontend mirror of the backend sample status state machine (labels + next-status preview; server is authoritative) | | `src/app/helpers/ag-grid/sample.tracking.config.ts` | **Our code** — Sample Tracking grid column config | | `src/app/helpers/services/tracking.service.ts` | **Our code** — tracking API client; `getProdOrderHeader`/licensing/lead-time plus the `getSamples`/sample CRUD/events/comments/photos/groups/boxes methods | | `Dockerfile` | Build pipeline: Node builder → nginx-brotli runtime | | `cloudbuild.yaml` | Google Cloud Build CI/CD pipeline | | `nginx.conf` | nginx serving config (SPA routing, brotli compression, caching) | | `angular.json` | Angular CLI build config (named `vex` — see §11) | | `package.json` | Dependencies and build scripts | | `dist/` | Build output — **never edit, never commit** | | `node_modules/` | Third-party packages — **never edit** | | `src/assets/` | Static images and icons | | `src/static-data/` | Static mock/lookup data files | | `src/CHANGELOG.md` | Version history | | `docs/` | Developer documentation | --- ## 4. The Prime Directive Our custom code lives here: - `src/app/pages/` - `src/app/helpers/` - `src/app/components/` - `src/app/custom-layout/` - `src/environments/` - `docs/` **Do not scatter business logic into `src/@vex/`.** The Vex framework provides layout, animation, and nav — it does not know about RFQ, item library, or margin formulas. Keep those concerns inside `src/app/pages/`. If you must modify a file outside the above directories, add it to the Core Modification Inventory below. --- ## 4a. Branch, sync, test, and PR workflow All feature work for this sandbox happens on `sandbox-albert`. Before adding new code: 1. Confirm the local branch is `sandbox-albert`. 2. Fetch GitHub. 3. Bring the latest `origin/develop` into GitHub's `sandbox-albert` by merging `origin/develop` into local `sandbox-albert`, then pushing `sandbox-albert` to GitHub. 4. Pull GitHub's `sandbox-albert` back into local `sandbox-albert`. 5. Only then add or change code. Branch rules: - Work only on `sandbox-albert`. - Use `develop` only as the source branch for syncing and the PR base. - Never commit to, merge into, or open PRs against `main`. - Do not force-push. Quality gate: - Add or update unit tests for every function or behavior you create or change. - Run the relevant frontend tests before committing; prefer the narrowest focused tests plus a TypeScript/build check when templates or services change. - **⚠️ Match the lockfile before trusting local checks.** Local `node_modules` can drift from `package.json`/`yarn.lock` (observed 2026-06-30: AG Grid **35.2.1** installed locally while the repo pins **36.0.0**). Looser local types let `tsc`/`jest` pass while the Docker build (`yarn test` on a fresh install) fails — i.e. green-locally / red-in-CI. Run `yarn install --immutable` to sync, especially after a dependency bump, before relying on any local `tsc`/`jest` result. The Docker build runs `yarn test`, so a clean local install is the only faithful CI reproduction. PR rule: - Commit locally on `sandbox-albert`, then push to `origin/sandbox-albert`. - Keep the GitHub PR for `sandbox-albert` -> `develop` updated after pushing. - If the PR does not exist, create it with `develop` as the base and `sandbox-albert` as the head. Commit identity: - Git `user.name`/`user.email` are intentionally unset in this repo. Set them per commit and use the GitHub **noreply** email — history is authored as `Albert Hazan `: `git -c user.name="Albert Hazan" -c user.email="u2giants@users.noreply.github.com" commit ...` - Pushing a commit authored with `u2giants@gmail.com` or `albert@popcre.com` is rejected with `GH007: Your push would publish a private email address` (account email-privacy protection). Recover with `git -c user.email="u2giants@users.noreply.github.com" commit --amend --reset-author --no-edit`, then push. --- ## 5. Core modification inventory The `src/@vex/` directory is committed into the repo but treated as upstream framework/layout code. Decision tree **Add or change a calculation (RFQ margin, landed cost, freight):** 1. Read `docs/rfq-math.md` first — it has all formulas, INCOTERM cost bases, and business context 2. Edit `src/app/pages/rfq/helpers/equation.ts` — cost helpers (`getFobTotalCost`, `getMddpTotalCost`, `getPoeTotalCost`, `getWhseTotalCost`, `getDeduction`, `getSellPrice`) are in the bottom half 3. `calculateLandedCost()` lives in `rfq.component.ts` (it needs access to `getMasterFiledName`) 4. Update `docs/rfq-math.md` if the formula changes 5. # RFQ Module

This is the primary feature module. It manages vendor requests-for-quote and computes the full cost chain from FOB factory price to retail sell price.

---

## File map

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `rfq.component.ts` | Master grid component. AG Grid setup, column wiring (valueSetters/valueGetters), dialog launches, row save/delete. ~4,100 lines. |
| `helpers/equation.ts` | All margin and sell-price calculations. Injectable service (`rfqEquation`). No AG Grid imports — pure business math. |
| `helpers/api-error.ts` | HTTP error message extraction (shared across the app, lives here for historical reasons). |
| `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts` | Pure helpers (no AG Grid imports, unit-tested): `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` snapshots duty rate + container price when a "Price for Sales" cell is edited; `buildSnapshotTooltip()` formats the tooltip text. |
| `price-sales-snapshot-renderer/` | Cell renderer for the 8 `*_price_sales` columns; renders the currency value and a native `title` hover tooltip built from the row's `price_sales_snapshots`. |
| `detail-cell-renderer/` | AG Grid master-detail renderer for vendor quotes. Each RFQ item expands to show its vendor rows. |
| `addNewProduct/` | Dialog for adding a new RFQ item (full form with all fields). |
| `addNewProductGrid/` | Alternative add-product flow using a mini-grid inside a dialog. |
| `duplicate_dialog/` | Duplicates an existing RFQ item (optionally copies vendors). |
| `container_cost_dialog/` | Dialog to set container cost (USD/container); triggers freight and landed-cost recalculation. |
| `duty_rate_dialog/` | Dialog to set the duty rate. |
| `multi_quote/` | Dialog to send quote requests to multiple vendors at once. |
| `autocomplete/` | Custom AG Grid cell editor for fields with server-loaded dropdown options. |
| `custom-dropdown/` | AG Grid cell editor for RFQ Group selection with add-new inline capability. |
| `numericEditor/` | AG Grid cell editor for numeric fields (validates > 0, < 1,000,000). |
| `datepicker/` | AG Grid cell editor for date fields. |
| `layout-dropdown/` | Toolbar component for saving/loading named grid view layouts. |
| `newVendor/` | Dialog to create a new vendor and assign it to an RFQ item. |
| `vendor/` | "Select Factories" dialog — attach one or more factories to an RFQ item. Factories already on the item are shown red + unclickable so staff can't add a duplicate (see "Detail grid" below). |

Grid column definitions live in **`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts`**, not in this folder. The pricing columns are wrapped into collapsible column groups by **`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq-column-groups.ts`** (see "Collapsible pricing column groups" below).

---

## Key concept: `getMasterFiledName(colId)`

Every piece of code that reads or writes to a row's data must go through `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the actual storage field name. This function iterates `this.colHeaders` (the column headers loaded from the backend) and returns the `field` value for a given `colId`.

- `colId` = stable lowercase-underscore identifier (e.g. `"fob_cost"`, `"cbm_pc"`)
- `field` = the backend storage key, which may contain spaces or special chars (e.g. `"FOB Cost"`, `"CBM/pc"`)

**Note:** The function name has a typo (`Filed` instead of `Field`). This is preserved intentionally — see AGENTS.md §11.

In `equation.ts`, the same function takes `colHeaders` as a second parameter: `getMasterFiledName(colId, colHeaders)`.

---

## Calculation pipeline

**For all pricing formulas, INCOTERM cost bases, deduction logic, and scenarios see [`docs/rfq-math.md`](../../../../docs/rfq-math.md). Only read that file when working on math — skip it otherwise.**

### Recalculation trigger chain

When a sourcing input is edited, the valueSetter in `rfq.component.ts` calls:

```
calculateLandedCost()        ← only if fob_cost / agent / duty / freight changed
recalculate_Lic_Gen()
  → setGenFobMargin          → gen_fob_sell_price
  → setGenMddpMargin         → gen_mddp_sell_price
  → setGenPoeMargin          → gen_poe_sell_price
  → setGenWhseMargin         → gen_whse_sell_price
  → setLicFobMargin          → lic_fob_sell_price
  → setLicMddpMargin         → lic_mddp_sell_price
  → setLicPoeMargin          → lic_poe_sell_price
  → setLicWhseMargin         → lic_whse_sell_price
```

All 8 setters run on every triggering edit to keep all columns in sync. Sell-price setters are only triggered when the user directly edits a sell-price cell.

---

## Price for Sales snapshot tooltips

The 8 `*_price_sales` columns are manually entered (the price sent to the sales team). A price-for-sales value is only meaningful relative to the **Duty Rate %** and **Container Price** at the time it was set — both of which drift. So when one of these cells is edited, `onCellValueChanged` calls `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` (in `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts`), which records `{ duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at }` into `params.data.price_sales_snapshots[colId]`. Clearing the cell removes that entry. The snapshot is persisted by the existing `updateRfqItem(params.data)` call (backend column `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`, TEXT/JSON).

The columns render through `priceSalesSnapshotRenderer`, which sets a native `title` tooltip from the stored snapshot so it always shows on hover. Rows with no recorded snapshot (legacy) show no tooltip. See AGENTS.md §11 for why a native tooltip is used here instead of the shared `customTooltip`.

---

## AG Grid patterns specific to this module

### Active status blockers and bilingual feedback

What changed:
Changing an RFQ row from Draft or Inactive to Active is blocked until every required launch field is present: Tech Pack Link, Case Pack, RFQ Group, Quantity, Customer, License, Description, and Delivery Location. `rfq.component.ts` passes the runtime field names from `getMasterFiledName()` into `helpers/rfq-status-validation.ts`; the validator returns `missingFields`, and both the snackbar and Status-cell native `title` tooltip show the exact missing list in English and Simplified Mandarin.

Why:
Users were blocked with a Tech Pack-only message even when another field was missing. The RFQ grid field names come from backend layout data, so the validator must receive runtime field names instead of hardcoding display labels.

Future sessions should:
When adding or removing Active-status prerequisites, update `getActiveStatusRequiredFields()` in `rfq.component.ts` and `rfq-status-validation.spec.ts`. Do not add a second ad hoc snackbar for one field; keep the missing-field list centralized so hover and click feedback stay identical.

### Silent blockers must explain the reason

What changed:
Common RFQ disabled/failure paths now surface bilingual feedback instead of only `console.error`: copy/new-round RFQ creation, vendor decline / Keep Price, factory bulk offer upload, and add-product option-load failures.

Why:
Production/vendor users can be blocked by missing group names, missing `.xlsx` files, inactive merch groups, failed dropdown loads, or backend copy guards. Console-only errors are invisible in normal use, and disabled Material buttons often need a wrapper element for tooltips to fire.

Future sessions should:
For any new disabled RFQ action, add a tooltip that names the missing prerequisite. For any caught API failure that changes user workflow, keep the `console.error` but also show a concise snackbar. If the disabled element is a Material button, wrap it in an inline element with `matTooltip`.

### Async valueSetters

Every valueSetter that calls `recalculate_Lic_Gen` is `async`. AG Grid v35 awaits the returned Promise — a rejection cancels the cell edit and reverts the value. Always:
- Return `true` on success
- Return `false` and show a snackbar on validation failure
- Do not let any exception escape (the try-catch in `recalculate_Lic_Gen` itself covers margin errors, but the outer setter must also be safe)

### Fill handle: keep drag-copy, block double-click fill-down

What changed:
The master RFQ grid still enables AG Grid fill-handle drag-copy through `cellSelection.handle: { mode: 'fill', direction: 'y', setFillValue }`. `rfq.component.ts` also installs `installFillHandleDoubleClickBlocker()` on grid ready, which catches `dblclick` events on `.ag-fill-handle` during the capture phase and stops AG Grid from auto-filling down the grid.

Why:
Double-clicking the selected-cell fill handle copied a Tech Pack Link value down the displayed grid and destroyed many row values. Users still need normal drag-to-copy, so removing the fill handle is not acceptable.

Future sessions should:
Do not "fix" this by setting `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or removing `cellSelection.handle`; that breaks drag-copy. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose the fill handle as an on/off feature and do not document a narrower double-click-only setting, so keep the event blocker unless AG Grid adds a first-class option. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag fill and double-click blocking.

### Range copy/paste across RFQ Groups

What changed:
The master RFQ grid uses AG Grid row grouping for `RFQ Group`, so a dragged cell range can visually cross gray group-header rows. AG Grid's default range-copy path walks displayed row indexes, which includes those group rows. `rfq.component.ts` now provides `sendToClipboard`; it lets AG Grid's default clipboard text pass through for normal ranges, but when a selected range contains group/header/detail/footer rows it rebuilds the copied tab-delimited data from real RFQ item rows only. Paste then lands row-for-row on real item rows instead of being offset by hidden/group rows. The fill-handle path also resolves dropdown values through `findDropdownObject()` first, using the AG Grid `rowNode` as the row-local fallback instead of scanning unrelated `rowData`.

Why:
Users drag-select down across different RFQ Groups and expect spreadsheet behavior: copied row 1 should paste into target row 1, copied row 2 into target row 2, regardless of group boundaries. Including group rows in the copied rectangle added blank/header rows to the clipboard and made paste appear shifted or mismatched.

Future sessions should:
Keep `sendToClipboard` scoped to the grouped-row correction. Do not replace AG Grid's clipboard behavior for plain ranges, and do not remove the existing dropdown `processCellForClipboard` / `processCellFromClipboard` conversion. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for grouped range copy, normal range pass-through, and dropdown fill value resolution.

### Tech Pack Link editing and opening

What changed:
The Tech Pack Link column renders through `renderTechPackCell()` instead of returning a raw `<a>` string. The cell shows the URL text plus explicit `Edit`, `Open`, and `Upload` buttons; normal clicks no longer navigate away.

Why:
When the whole cell was an anchor, users could not reliably select/edit/delete URL text because clicking the cell opened a new browser tab. The explicit buttons keep opening/uploading available while leaving AG Grid text editing accessible via double-click or the `Edit` button.

Future sessions should:
Keep Tech Pack Link navigation behind an explicit control. If changing this renderer, verify that users can still edit/delete the text value and that opening the link requires clicking `Open`.

### Native AG Grid undo

What changed:
RFQ uses AG Grid's native undo stack (`undoRedoCellEditing: true`, limit `50`), and the toolbar Undo button calls `gridApi.undoCellEditing()`. The old custom undo path that fetched all RFQ rows on every edit was removed.

Why:
AG Grid records edits, fill-handle operations, paste, delete, undo, and redo through `cellValueChanged`; the custom snapshot restore did not reliably cover fill/copy/paste and could restore stale server data.

Future sessions should:
Keep persistence in `onCellValueChanged` so undo operations save the reverted row. If adding custom edit flows, make sure they use normal AG Grid value changes (`setDataValue`, valueSetter, or editor commit) so the undo stack can see them.

### Column identity: never hardcode field names

```typescript
// Correct
params.data[this.getMasterFiledName('cbm_pc')]

// Wrong
params.data['CBM/pc']
```

### Collapsible pricing column groups

The 28 pricing columns form 8 collapsible AG Grid column groups — the cross of
{Gen, Lic} × {FOB, POE, Whse, mDDP}. "Price for Sales" stays visible when a group
is collapsed; the other columns (`columnGroupShow: 'open'`) show only when expanded.
Groups start expanded (`openByDefault: true`).

- **Grouping is a transform, not config.** `rfq.grid.config.ts` stays a **flat** array.
  `helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq-column-groups.ts` (`groupRfqPricingColumns`) wraps the
  pricing columns into `ColGroupDef`s as the **last** step when building `columnDefs`
  in `rfq.component.ts` — *after* role filtering and auto-formatters run on the flat
  list. `colHeaders` stays flat (the layout dropdown and saved-view ordering depend on
  it); only the grid's `columnDefs` is nested. It groups by the `group-header-*`
  `headerClass` marker already on each pricing column.
- **Group open/closed state persists separately from column state.** AG Grid keeps it
  in its own structure (`getColumnGroupState()`/`setColumnGroupState()` → `[{groupId,
  open}]`), so it is NOT part of `getColumnState()`. It rides along in the existing Save
  View: captured in `saveRfqViewState()` (used by both "create view" and "save current
  view") and re-applied in `applyGridViewStateForViewName()`. Stored server-side in
  `GridViewState.column_group_state` (JSONB).

### Duty rate: always use optional chaining

The duty rate can be stored as `{ title: "7.5" }` (object from autocomplete) or as a raw numeric string. Always:

```typescript
const dutyRateRaw = data[this.getMasterFiledName('duty_rate')];
const duty_rate = dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0;
```

### cellDataType: false on numeric string columns

Any column that uses `numericCellEditor` AND whose backend data is stored as strings **must** have `cellDataType: false` in its runtime setup block. Without it, AG Grid v35 infers the column type as `'text'` and issues warning `#135`, silently reverting the edit before the valueSetter is called.

```typescript
} else if (colHeaders[i].colId == "cbm_pc") {
  colHeaders[i].cellDataType = false;        // ← prevents warning #135
  colHeaders[i]['cellEditor'] = 'numericCellEditor';
  colHeaders[i].valueSetter = async (params) => { ... };
}
```

Do NOT use `cellDataType: 'number'` — that applies AG Grid's built-in number formatter and shows "invalid number" for string-valued cells loaded from the backend.

The `numericCellEditor` already enforces number-only input via `getValue()` returning `null` for any non-parseable value, so `cellDataType: false` does not open the door to bad data.

### AG Grid deprecated APIs — what NOT to use

| Removed | Replacement | Removed in |
|---|---|---|
| `params.column.userProvidedColDef` | `params.column.getColDef()` | AG Grid v35 |
| `params.columnApi` | `params.api` | AG Grid v31 |
| `getRowNodeId` | `getRowId` | AG Grid v28 |
| `observable.toPromise()` | `firstValueFrom(observable)` | RxJS 7 (deprecated) |

---

## Toolbar and header layout (heights are deliberately tuned)

- **Header-row heights are set via the grid API, not just CSS.** `gridOptions` in
  `rfq.component.ts` sets `groupHeaderHeight: 32`, `headerHeight: 48`,
  `floatingFiltersHeight: 40`. `rfq.component.scss` also pins the matching per-row-type
  heights (`.ag-header-row-group/-column/-filter`). **Keep the two in sync** — AG Grid sizes
  the `.ag-header` container from the API values, so a CSS-only change leaves a tall empty gap
  under the filter row (the container stays at the theme default while the rows shrink).
  `headerHeight: 48` is the floor that still fits the tallest 3-line wrapped title
  (e.g. "Gen mDDP Price For Sales", ~47px) — don't drop it below that without also narrowing
  the wrap. Note the floating-filter row class in v36 is `ag-header-row-filter` (not the old
  `ag-header-row-floating-filter`).
- **Toolbar field centering:** the `mat-select` toolbar fields (`.toolbar-field`) set
  `--mat-form-field-container-height: 34px`. That token is load-bearing — Material centers the
  outline's resting label at container-height / 2, so leaving it at the default 56px while the
  infix is flex-shrunk drops the empty label (e.g. "Status") to the bottom of the box.
- **Detail header must not inherit the master header height.** The detail grid renders *inside*
  `.grid-wrapper`, so the master rule `.grid-wrapper .ag-header-row-column { height: 48px }`
  leaks into it and overflows its 40px `.ag-header` (visually cutting off the first sub-grid
  row). `detail-cell-renderer.component.scss` pins `.ag-header .ag-header-row` back to 40px with
  higher specificity to block the leak.

## Detail grid (vendor quotes)

Each RFQ item row expands to show vendor quotes in a detail grid managed by `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`. The detail grid:

- Loads its own column definitions from the backend (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`)
- Has its own `onCellValueChanged` that calls `updateVendor(data)` on every change
- Uses `addDetailGridInfo` / `removeDetailGridInfo` for master-detail grid registration
- The `price_code` column change in the detail grid sets `quote_accepted = true` on the vendor row
- Default-sorts by factory price ascending — see "Default sort" below

### Default sort: lowest price first, unpriced/declined last

`configureDetailColumnHeaders()` calls `applyDefaultPriceSort(colHeaders)` after building the
column defs. This makes the **price column the primary default sort** (`sort: 'asc'`,
`sortIndex: 0`); the `name` column stays as the secondary alphabetical tiebreaker (`sortIndex: 1`).

A custom comparator on the price column ranks rows so that any quote with no usable price
(blank / zero / negative `Price`) or where the factory **declined to quote** (`req_status == 1`,
set by the "pass" button in `rfq-button.component.ts`) is sunk to the bottom regardless of value;
real quotes are ordered lowest-price-first above them. The comparator reads `node.data.Price`
directly (via `isUnpricedOrDeclined()` / `toPrice()`), so it does not depend on the column's
`colId`, `field`, or `valueGetter`.

**Why `applyDefaultPriceSort` searches for the colId instead of hardcoding it:** the backend
`rfq_detail` layout serves the visible price column under colId **`price`** (legacy), while the
config file `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` uses `quote_price`. The helper targets whichever of the
two the layout actually provides, preferring the visible one. The first attempt (`e8ba0551`)
hardcoded `quote_price` and silently did nothing because the live column is `price`; fixed in
`b241ce79`. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Detail-grid default sort targets the live price colId, not a hardcoded one"`.

### Vendor quote rows and the duplicate-factory guard

Each item's quotes live on the master row's **`Vendors`** array (capital V — Sequelize association name); each element is `{ Vendor: { id, ... }, Price, Note, Status, quote_id, ... }`. The same `Vendor.id` may appear more than once:

- **Allowed (factory side):** the detail sub-grid "Add Quote" button lets a factory submit a second quote line for the item, as long as a note explains why the price differs. This path is intentionally **unguarded**.
- **Blocked (staff side):** the "Add Factory" → "Select Factories" dialog (`vendor/`) must not let sourcing/production/admin add a factory that is already on the item. `onBtnNewVendor()` builds `existingVendorIds` from `node.data.Vendors.map(v => v?.Vendor?.id)` and passes them as `DialogData.vendors`; the dialog renders those factories red and unclickable.

The guard is **UI-only** — there is no DB unique constraint or backend dedupe on `(RFQ item, vendor)`, and it depends on the exact capital-`V` `Vendors` casing. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Same factory twice on one RFQ item"`.

### Re-quote a stale price (orange flag + Keep Price)

When a factory's price is old, internal Sourcing/admin can right-click the detail-grid price cell → **"Request new quote"** (`getContextMenuItems`, gated to `admin`/`sourcing_manager`/`production`). That calls `itemService.requestRequote({ quote_id, priceCode })`, which sets `RFQVendor.requote_requested = true` and emails the factory. The flag drives, in **both** the staff and vendor views:

- an **orange** price cell — `cellClassRules: { 'requote-requested-cell': … }`, added to **both** the `quote_price` and legacy `price` colId branches (the live layout may serve either — same reason as the default-sort helper above);
- a **bilingual tooltip** `报价 - Quoted <Month D YYYY>` via `tooltipValueGetter`, using the original `quote_date` (which `requestRequote` leaves untouched).

The vendor clears it two ways: typing a **new price** (the `quote_price` valueSetter sets `requote_requested = false` locally; backend clears it on save) or clicking the **保留价格 - Keep Price** button (`rfq-button.component.ts` `onKeepPrice()` → `itemService.keepPrice({ quote_id })`, which clears the flag and refreshes `quote_date` to today). Vendor-facing strings are inline `中文 - English` (no i18n framework — see `AGENTS.md §11`). The backend guards both mutating endpoints with `vendorMayModifyQuote` so a vendor can't act on another factory's quote via `quote_id`.

---

## Window globals (known tech debt)

`window['itemService']` references in toolbar callbacks were resolved in commit `40b3ac9` — the service is now captured at closure-creation time in `getContextMenuItems()`.

Remaining `window` globals in `rfq.component.ts` (`window['router']`, `window['gridApi']`, `window['base64Images']`, `window['togglePageToolbar']`) are used for inter-callback communication in contexts where Angular DI is not available. **Do not add new `window['...']` references.**

Done. I documented the fix in the narrow RFQ module doc, not in global `AGENTS.md`:

[RFQ README](D:/repos/designflow-frontend/src/app/pages/rfq/README.md)
